title: Can ChatGPT assist in cost analysis and bid pricing in construction estimating? A pilot study using a bridge rehabilitation project
inputData: 文本（项目描述+专家知识）
contribution: 首次评估ChatGPT-4在建设成本估算和投标定价中的应用潜力，通过Prompt Engineering方法初步验证LLM在施工成本分析中的可行性
abstract: This study evaluates the efficacy of ChatGPT-4 in analyzing construction work item costs and estimating bid prices, using the rehabilitation of the Beaver Dam Road Bridge in Pennsylvania, USA as a case study. A custom GPT was developed with a knowledge base of production rates, labor wages, equipment rates, and material costs to estimate bid prices for five construction items across ten repeated trials. The results show that ChatGPT-4 achieved an overall Total Bid Package accuracy of 99.84% compared to ground truth, though individual item MAPE varied from 0.17% to 17.48%, highlighting both the potential and consistency limitations of LLMs in construction cost estimating.
methodology: A custom GPT was configured with a knowledge base containing production rates, hourly labor wages, equipment rates, and material costs for five construction items from a bridge rehabilitation project, and step-by-step prompting instructions guided ChatGPT-4 through cost estimation calculations. The model was evaluated over ten repeated trials per item, with outputs compared against ground truth manual calculations and real bid data from three PennDOT bidders.
results: ChatGPT-4 achieved a Total Bid Package accuracy of 99.84% against ground truth ($61,009.68 vs. $60,910.14), with individual item MAPE ranging from 0.17% (reinforcement labor) to 17.48% (membrane waterproofing unit bid price). The model occasionally produced N/A outputs instead of numerical estimates (6 out of 50 trials, approximately 12%), indicating consistency and reliability issues that require further investigation.
